London, United Kingdom

ProLuxe Bureau

Run a 200-person hotel programme without chasing 200 people.

Bureau is the console we built for our office. Each guest gets a personal link, confirms their own room, and the status updates live. You see the programme, not the inbox.

01 / Guests confirm their own rooms.

Guests confirm their own rooms.

Each guest receives a personal link, picks dates and preferences, confirms in under 2 minutes. No account creation, no app download. You stop chasing.

02 / Rooms held until the deadline.

Rooms held until the deadline.

Daily inventory holds protect your block. Unconfirmed rooms release on a schedule you set, not a hotel surprise. No more 6pm scrambles the day before.

03 / One status view across every guest.

One status view across every guest.

Confirmed, pending, dropped. Filter by hotel, by date, by guest. Export the rooming list when you need it. The hotel works from the same record you do.

How it works for a programme

Three steps from a configured programme to a live dashboard. We set the shape, your guests fill it in, your team handles edits in place or passes them back.

01

We configure Bureau before your event opens.

Rooming categories, daily holds, hotel contracts, all reflecting what we have already negotiated. The limits are in place before any guest sees a link. Your team logs in to a programme that already knows the rules.

bureau.proluxetravels.com / programme-demo / configure
Hotels
3 properties
Room categories
Standard, Superior
Daily hold
200 rooms
Booking window
Day 1 to Day 4
Release schedule
10 per day from cutoff
Contracts
Signed, on file

02

You send each guest their personal sign-in link.

No account, no app. The guest opens the link, picks dates and a room from the approved shortlist, and confirms. The dashboard updates the moment they submit, and the rooming list reconciles itself.

mail / inbox / personal-link
From ProLuxe Travel
Your hotel room for the programme
Dear A. Patel, your personal link below holds your room until the deadline. Pick dates and room, confirm, done.
Confirm my room →
Held until cutoff One link per guest

03

If someone needs a hand, you adjust in place, or pass it back.

Direct edits, a date shift inside the event window, switch between approved hotels, fresh confirmation, take 2 clicks on the dashboard. Anything outside your agreed options passes to ProLuxe Travel with one click and a pre-filled reason. The history shows every action either way.

bureau.proluxetravels.com / booking / amend
Amend booking, A. Patel
Day 1
Day 4
Hotel A · Standard
Outside event window? Pass to ProLuxe → We handle it directly

Hands-on, or hands-off

Some bookers run the programme themselves through Bureau, sending the personal links, adjusting bookings in place, exporting the rooming list when they need it. Others send us the rooming list and monitor the dashboard as we work through it. Same console, same audit trail. Your call.

When the deadline matters

Daily inventory hold is set when we configure the programme. Rooms stay held against your block until the date you set. After that, unconfirmed rooms release back to the hotel on a schedule, not a phone call.

You see the count drop in real time. The hotel sees the same. No reconciling, no late surprises.

bureau.proluxetravels.com/programmes/[programme]/inventory
Hotel A
Host city
Held
80
rooms held
200 total programme
Release schedule
Held
Releasing 10/day from cutoff
Released
Held until the cutoff you set. After that, 10 rooms release per day through the tail of the window.

What it does

The capabilities your programme team sees on day one.

A quote-acceptance step that locks the selection before the dashboard opens (booker-built for principal travel, shortlisted for programmes), groups per cohort with two booking modes, a restricted guest view per booking, edits within the options you set, and one live status grid.

01

Quote acceptance, before the dashboard opens.

For principal travel, your booker builds the combination they want: hotels per leg, room types with their own quantities, dates per stay, multiple combinations saved side by side. For programmes and conferences, we shortlist two or three options and the booker picks one primary, the others as fallback. Either way, the dashboard then opens with the approved selection locked in. From there you split the allocation into groups for VIPs, speakers, staff, and guests, and decide how each group books.

bureau.proluxetravels.com / programme-demo / quotes
Option A Hotel A 200 rooms · 3 nights
Walk to venue Primary
Option B Hotel B 200 rooms · 3 nights
10 min by car Fallback
Option C Hotel C 200 rooms · 3 nights
Boutique Mark

02

Two modes per group. Pick the right one per cohort.

For VIPs and staff, you upload the rooming list and the bookings appear. For guests, you generate personal sign-in links and each one books within the limits you set. The two modes coexist in the same programme, and used-vs-allocated numbers against each group update live as bookings land.

mail / inbox
From ProLuxe Travel
Your hotel room for the programme
Dear A. Patel, your personal link below holds your room until the deadline.
Confirm my room →

03

Guests see one booking, theirs. Nothing else.

When a guest clicks their sign-in link, they land on a single-booking view. Reference number plus surname unlocks it. They see their hotel, their room, their dates, and three actions: resend confirmation, change dates, pass to ProLuxe Travel. No programme view, no other guests, no account to create.

bureau / booking / amend
Amend booking
Day 1
Day 4
Hotel A · Standard
Need something outside this? Pass to ProLuxe
→ We handle it directly

04

One overview, five numbers above the fold.

Block utilisation across the event. Days to cutoff and to first arrival. Outstanding actions. Rooms used per hotel. Booking pace this week with a delta against last. The five numbers a programme manager opens in the morning to know exactly where the programme stands, before scrolling to the list of guests who haven't yet booked.

bureau / programme-demo / rooming
GuestHotelDay
A. PatelHotel A1 / 4
J. RouxHotel B1 / 3
M. LinHotel C2 / 4
S. KowalHotel A1 / 4
D. OkaforHotel D1 / 2

Intelligence, optional add-on

Pre-event briefs, live monitoring, board-ready reports.

For programmes that need it, we work with Colossus Intelligence to ship a protective intelligence cycle alongside the booking work. Threat matrix, zone classifications, pre-event briefs, and live monitoring around the event window. The briefs sit on the same dashboard your team uses, with both the operational and board-ready versions a click away. Scoped and quoted per programme, separate from the standard Bureau engagement.

Always on hand

Same record, same handoff. If a booking needs us, a complex amendment, a last-minute relocation, a payment question, pass it back. We pick it up, the history shows the handoff, and you carry on with the next 50 guests.

If you are running a programme of 50 or more guests in 2026, we should talk. We take a small number of new programmes per quarter.

Speak with us

Corporate & luxury travel management.
